# My LeetCode Practice

> [中文版](./README.zh.md)

A C++17 project for practicing coding problems (LeetCode, OJ, etc.).

## How It Works

**You only write the solution logic.** Everything else — declarations, data structures, test cases — is auto-generated by the AI agent (opencode).

```
User: "帮我实现 LeetCode 112 题 路径总和"
Agent:  →  include/solution.h    (declaration)
         →  include/tree.ai.h    (TreeNode struct)
         →  src/solution.cpp     (stub, you fill it in)
         →  tests/solution_test.ai.cpp (test cases)

You:   Edit src/solution.cpp → implement the method
       Ctrl+Shift+B          → compile & run tests
       F5                    → debug
```

## Quick Start

### 1. Tell the Agent

```bash
# In opencode:
implement problem LeetCode 112 Path Sum
# Or:
帮我实现 LeetCode 112
```

The agent will generate all necessary files.

### 2. Solve the Problem

Open `src/solution.cpp` and fill in your solution.

### 3. Build, Run & Debug

| Action | Shortcut | What happens |
|---|---|---|
| **Build only** | `Ctrl+Shift+B` → select "build_lc" | `make` → `solution_test` |
| **Build + Run** | `Ctrl+Shift+B` → select "run_lc" | `make` → `./solution_test` |
| **Debug** | `F5` | `make` → launch GDB on `solution_test` |

## File Naming Convention

- `*.ai.*` — AI-generated files. **Do not edit manually.** Any edits will be overwritten when generating a new problem.
- `solution.h` / `solution.cpp` — User interface. The declaration is generated, the implementation is yours.
- All other files — project infrastructure (Makefile, README, etc.).

## Supported Platforms

- **LeetCode** — Implements the `Solution` class interface. Agent generates data structures (`TreeNode`, `ListNode`, etc.) and gtest cases.
- **OJ platforms** (POJ, Codeforces, etc.) — The problem's I/O is mapped to a function signature. Agent generates gtest cases that simulate stdin/stdout.
